key_items: Bitcoin rises above $62,000 after CPI report gives traders relief from $60,000 breakdown test
Bank of Japan rate decision looms with historical 22.5% sell-off average on rate hikes
Institutional adoption accelerating: TradFi rushing into crypto, Fidelity largest Ethereum purchase in 2 months, Tether-led $1.4B NEURA funding
reasoning: Mixed signals dominate the near-term outlook: supportive factors include institutional accumulation on dips, positive inflation data providing relief, and regulatory clarity efforts (CLARITY Act, CFTC prediction markets framework), offsetting technical resistance weakness and BOJ policy uncertainty. However, falling retail demand, layer-2 network shutdowns (Botanix), and security incidents (Humanity Protocol) create headwinds that prevent a stronger bullish lean over the 1-7 day swing window.
risks: Bank of Japan rate hike decision could trigger a sharp 15-20% pullback if hawkish, and weak demand despite lower prices suggests institutional buying may not sustain rallies above technical resistance at $62,000-$63,000.
